file.content:
const { createJsWithBabelPreset } = require('ts-jest');

const jsWithBabelPreset = createJsWithBabelPreset({
  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.spec.json',
  babelConfig: true,
});

module.exports = {
  preset: 'react-native',
  moduleFileExtensions: [
    'ts',
    'tsx',
    'js',
    'jsx',
    'json',
    'node',
  ],
  clearMocks: true,
  transform: jsWithBabelPreset.transform,
  transformIgnorePatterns: [],
  setupFiles: [],
  verbose: true,
  setupFilesAfterEnv: ['<rootDir>/__tests__/setup.js'],
  testMatch: [
    '<rootDir>/**/*.test.js',
    '<rootDir>/**/*.test.ts',
    '<rootDir>/**/*.test.tsx',
  ],
  testPathIgnorePatterns: [],
  coverageDirectory: '__tests__/coverage',
  moduleDirectories: [
    'node_modules',
    'src',
    '<rootDir>/__tests__/utils/test-utils.tsx',
  ],
  testResultsProcessor: 'jest-sonar-reporter',
};

    11→      "~/store": ["./@types/store.d.ts"]
    12→    },
    13→    "lib": ["es2020"],
    14→    "typeRoots": [

    19→    "noEmit": true,
    20→    "strict": true,
    21→    "target": "esnext",
    22→    "strictNullChecks": true,
    23→    "skipLibCheck": true,

    28→    "./src",

file.content:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "allowJs": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "baseUrl": "./",
    "rootDir": "./",
    "jsx": "react-native",
    "paths": {
      "~/store": ["./@types/store.d.ts"]
    },
    "lib": ["es2020"],
    "typeRoots": [
      "./@types",
      "./node_modules/@types"
    ],
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "noEmit": true,
    "strict": true,
    "target": "esnext",
    "strictNullChecks": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true
  },
  "include": [
    "./src",
    "./__tests__",
    "./@types",
    "./scripts"
  ],
  "exclude": [
    "node_modules",
    "babel.config.js",
    "metro.config.js",
    "jest.config.js"
  ]
}

file.content:
{
  "name": "@semparar/mf-app-fintech-rewardprogram",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "main": "./src/index.ts",
  "description": "Sem Parar Rewards Program",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/SemParar-B2C/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram.git"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "android": "react-native run-android",
    "ios": "react-native run-ios",
    "start": "react-native start",
    "test": "jest --maxWorkers=50% --passWithNoTests",
    "bump-patch": "npm version patch --no-git-tag-version && bundle exec fastlane bump",
    "bump-minor": "npm version minor --no-git-tag-version && bundle exec fastlane bump",
    "bump-major": "npm version major --no-git-tag-version && bundle exec fastlane bump",
    "sync:yalc": "nodemon --watch src --ext js,ts,tsx,json --exec \"yalc publish --push\"",
    "sync-typings": "ts-node scripts/sync-typings.ts",
    "knip": "knip",
    "knip:fix": "knip --fix --allow-remove-files",
    "lint": "run-s lint:*",
    "lint:eslint": "eslint . --fix --cache --format ./scripts/core/eslint-formatter-sorted.js",
    "lint:prettier": "prettier --write --ignore-unknown .",
    "lint:typescript": "ts-node ./scripts/typescript-check.ts",
    "prepare": "husky install"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "@hookform/resolvers": "^2.9.10",
    "@noble/curves": "^2.0.1",
    "@noble/hashes": "^2.0.1",
    "@react-native-async-storage/async-storage": "^1.23.1",
    "@react-navigation/native": "^6.0.10",
    "@react-navigation/native-stack": "^6.6.2",
    "@semparar/design-system-app": "^3.39.15",
    "@semparar/mf-app-fraudprevention": "^1.0.8",
    "@semparar/mf-app-user": "^0.32.3",
    "axios": "^1.7.7",
    "babel-plugin-module-resolver": "^4.1.0",
    "crypto-es": "*",
    "immer": "^10.2.0",
    "jwt-decode": "^3.1.2",
    "lottie-react-native": "^6.7.2",
    "metro-react-native-babel-preset": "^0.76.8",
    "react-native-config": "^1.5.5",
    "react-native-device-info": "^8.7.0",
    "react-native-get-random-values": "^1.11.0",
    "react-native-modal": "^13.0.0",
    "react-native-safe-area-context": "^4.10.7",
    "react-native-screens": "^3.33.0",
    "react-native-user-agent": "^2.3.1",
    "react-native-video": "^5.0.2",
    "react-native-webview": "^13.6.2",
    "react-redux": "^8.0.1",
    "react-test-renderer": "17.0.2",
    "reactotron-react-native": "^5.0.1",
    "reactotron-redux": "^3.1.3",
    "reactotron-redux-saga": "^4.2.3",
    "redux": "^4.2.0",
    "redux-persist": "^6.0.0",
    "redux-saga": "^1.1.3",
    "text-encoding": "^0.7.0",
    "yup": "^0.32.11",
    "zustand": "^5.0.8"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@babel/core": "^7.24.7",
    "@babel/plugin-transform-class-properties": "^7.24.7",
    "@babel/plugin-transform-export-namespace-from": "^7.27.1",
    "@babel/plugin-transform-flow-strip-types": "^7.24.7",
    "@babel/plugin-transform-private-methods": "^7.24.7",
    "@babel/plugin-transform-private-property-in-object": "^7.22.11",
    "@babel/plugin-transform-template-literals": "^7.27.1",
    "@commitlint/cli": "^17.1.2",
    "@commitlint/config-conventional": "^17.1.0",
    "@noble/curves": "^2.0.1",
    "@noble/hashes": "^2.0.1",
    "@react-native-async-storage/async-storage": "^1.15.14",
    "@react-native-community/eslint-config": "^2.0.0",
    "@react-navigation/native": "^6.1.9",
    "@react-navigation/native-stack": "^6.9.15",
    "@semparar/design-system-app": "^3.39.15",
    "@semparar/mf-app-fraudprevention": "^1.0.8",
    "@semparar/mf-app-user": "^0.32.3",
    "@testing-library/jest-native": "^5.4.3",
    "@testing-library/react-native": "^13.3.3",
    "@types/bun": "latest",
    "@types/jest": "^29.5.0",
    "@types/react": "^18.0.24",
    "@types/react-native": "^0.67.3",
    "@types/semver": "^7.7.1",
    "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin": "^8.34.1",
    "@typescript-eslint/parser": "^8.34.1",
    "axios": "^1.7.7",
    "babel-plugin-module-resolver": "^4.1.0",
    "babel-plugin-root-import": "^6.6.0",
    "commitlint": "^20.3.1",
    "crypto-es": "^2.0.4",
    "date-fns": "^3.6.0",
    "eslint": "^9.29.0",
    "eslint-config-airbnb": "^19.0.4",
    "eslint-config-prettier": "^10.1.5",
    "eslint-import-resolver-babel-module": "^5.3.2",
    "eslint-import-resolver-babel-plugin-root-import": "^1.1.1",
    "eslint-import-resolver-node": "^0.3.9",
    "eslint-import-resolver-typescript": "^4.4.3",
    "eslint-plugin-import": "^2.31.0",
    "eslint-plugin-import-helpers": "^1.2.1",
    "eslint-plugin-jest": "^28.14.0",
    "eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y": "^6.5.1",
    "eslint-plugin-prettier": "^5.5.0",
    "eslint-plugin-react": "^7.37.5",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^5.2.0",
    "eslint-plugin-react-native": "^5.0.0",
    "husky": "^8.0.1",
    "immer": "^10.2.0",
    "jest": "^29.7.0",
    "jest-sonar-reporter": "^2.0.0",
    "jwt-decode": "^3.1.2",
    "knip": "^5.82.1",
    "lint-staged": "^13.2.2",
    "lottie-react-native": "^6.7.2",
    "metro-react-native-babel-preset": "0.76.8",
    "moment": "^2.29.4",
    "nodemon": "^3.1.11",
    "npm-run-all": "^4.1.5",
    "prettier": "^3.8.1",
    "prettier-plugin-organize-imports": "^4.3.0",
    "react": "^18.2.0",
    "react-native": "^0.73.6",
    "react-native-device-info": "^5.3.1",
    "react-native-get-random-values": "^1.11.0",
    "react-native-reanimated": "3.7.0",
    "react-native-safe-area-context": "^4.10.7",
    "react-native-screens": "^3.32.0",
    "react-native-user-agent": "^2.3.1",
    "react-native-video": "^5.0.2",
    "react-native-webview": "^13.6.2",
    "react-redux": "^7.2.4",
    "react-test-renderer": "^18.3.1",
    "reactotron-react-native": "5.1.10",
    "reactotron-redux": "^3.1.10",
    "reactotron-redux-saga": "^4.2.3",
    "redux": "^4.1.0",
    "redux-persist": "^6.0.0",
    "redux-saga": "^1.1.3",
    "semver": "^7.7.3",
    "styled-components": "^6.1.14",
    "text-encoding": "^0.7.0",
    "ts-jest": "^29.4.6",
    "ts-node": "^10.9.2",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.34.1",
    "yalc": "^1.0.0-pre.53",
    "zustand": "^5.0.8",
    "zx": "^8.8.5"
  },
  "rnpm": {
    "assets": [
      "./src/assets/fonts/"
    ]
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.{jsx,ts,tsx,json}": [
      "npm run lint"
    ]
  }
}

1→module.exports = {

content.text: Now let me check how `createJsWithBabelPreset` actually generates the transform map and verify the transform output covers all file extensions.
Now let me check the `moduleResolution: "bundler"` concern. This is a significant setting that ts-jest will inherit from `tsconfig.spec.json` which extends `tsconfig.json`.

Now I have everything I need for a thorough review. Let me compile my findings.

---

# Pull Request Review: Task 13 -- Configure ts-jest to run typechecks during tests

## Verdict: **APPROVED** with minor comments

---

### 1. Correctness of ts-jest configuration

**PASS.** The choice of `createJsWithBabelPreset` is exactly right for this project.

Looking at the source code in `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/node_modules/ts-jest/dist/presets/create-jest-preset.js` (line 71-78), this function generates:

```js
{
  transform: {
    '^.+\\.jsx?$': 'babel-jest',       // JS_TRANSFORM_PATTERN
    '^.+\\.tsx?$': ['ts-jest', opts],   // TS_TRANSFORM_PATTERN
  }
}
```

This means:
- `.js` / `.jsx` files continue through `babel-jest` (which the `react-native` preset was already using for everything). This is critical because `node_modules` in React Native projects ship raw JS/JSX that must go through Babel.
- `.ts` / `.tsx` files go through `ts-jest`, which performs actual TypeScript type-checking before compilation.

The `babelConfig: true` flag is correct -- it tells ts-jest to load `babel.config.js` after TypeScript compilation, which is essential because `babel.config.js` contains the `module-resolver` plugin that resolves the `~` alias (line 27-28 of `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/babel.config.js`).

The `tsconfig: 'tsconfig.spec.json'` reference is also correct.

**Package version:** `ts-jest@^29.4.6` paired with `jest@^29.7.0` -- version alignment is solid.

---

### 2. jest.mock() fixes assessment

**PASS.** Both fixes follow the correct pattern.

**`AirlineMilesMarketplace.test.tsx`** (line 29-38): The `require('react')` inside the factory + `React.createElement` instead of JSX is the canonical solution. This is necessary because `jest.mock()` factories are hoisted to the top of the file by Jest's transform. With ts-jest using `"jsx": "react"`, JSX in `.tsx` files compiles to `React.createElement(...)`, but the `React` import (which is a top-level ESM import) gets hoisted *below* the `jest.mock()` factory after transformation. Using `require('react')` inside the factory avoids this hoisting issue entirely.

**`MyOrdersRewardProgram.test.tsx`** (line 107-136): Same pattern, properly applied to the more complex FlatList mock. The explicit `(props: any)` and `(item: any, index: number)` type annotations are a minor improvement for ts-jest's type-checking pass, but more importantly they prevent `noImplicitAny` errors if that flag is ever enabled.

**`setup.js`** at `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/__tests__/setup.js`: This file is `.js`, not `.ts`, so it correctly routes through `babel-jest` and does not need the same treatment. Several mocks in this file already use the `require` + `React.createElement` pattern (e.g., lines 44-74, 143-152), confirming the established convention.

---

### 3. tsconfig.spec.json approach

**PASS with one comment.**

The override `"jsx": "react"` is the correct and necessary change. The base `tsconfig.json` uses `"jsx": "react-native"`, which emits JSX as-is (no transformation). ts-jest running in Node needs JSX compiled to `React.createElement`, so `"jsx": "react"` is required.

**COMMENT (non-blocking):** The base `tsconfig.json` has `"moduleResolution": "bundler"`. This is inherited by `tsconfig.spec.json`. While `moduleResolution: "bundler"` is generally fine for ts-jest (it allows extension-less imports, which is the norm in React Native), be aware that:

- `moduleResolution: "bundler"` permits `import` conditions from `package.json` `exports` fields. If a dependency exposes different entry points for `import` vs `require`, TypeScript will resolve the `import` condition, but Jest (running in CJS mode) will resolve the `require` condition. This could cause type mismatches in rare cases.
- This is a pre-existing condition, not introduced by this PR, so it does not block approval.

**COMMENT (non-blocking):** The base tsconfig has `"noUnusedLocals": true` and `"noUnusedParameters": true`. Since `tsconfig.spec.json` inherits these, test files will be type-checked with these strict rules. This is generally desirable but could cause friction if test helpers declare unused params (e.g., `(_event: Event)` placeholders). Given that all 113 tests currently pass, this is a non-issue today but worth noting for future test authors.

---

### 4. Transform configuration coverage

**PASS.**

The resulting transform map:
```
'^.+\\.jsx?$' -> babel-jest
'^.+\\.tsx?$' -> ts-jest (with babelConfig: true, tsconfig: tsconfig.spec.json)
```

Coverage analysis:
- **All project test files** are `.test.ts` or `.test.tsx` (confirmed via `testMatch` in `jest.config.js` line 24-28). These match `^.+\\.tsx?$` and route through ts-jest. Correct.
- **`__tests__/setup.js`** is a `.js` file. It matches `^.+\\.jsx?$` and routes through babel-jest. Correct.
- **`node_modules/` JS files**: `transformIgnorePatterns: []` (line 20) means ALL node_modules are transformed. This is standard for React Native projects where many packages ship untranspiled source. The `.js`/`.jsx` files go through babel-jest. Correct.
- **No `.test.js` files exist** in the project (confirmed by glob), so there is no risk of JS test files silently bypassing type-checking.

The `preset: 'react-native'` at line 9 is maintained. Note that when `transform` is explicitly provided, it **overrides** the preset's transform entirely (Jest does not merge). This is the intended behavior since the new transform map fully covers the same file extensions.

---

### 5. Regression and risk analysis

**Low risk. No regressions identified.**

- **All 113 tests pass.** This is the primary validation.
- **No behavioral change for existing JS files.** They still go through babel-jest.
- **TypeScript files gain type-checking.** This is purely additive -- it catches more errors, it does not change runtime behavior.
- **Performance impact:** ts-jest with type-checking is slower than babel-jest (roughly 2-3x for the type-checking pass). For 17 test suites, this should be negligible. ts-jest caches compiled output in `node_modules/.cache/ts-jest/`, so subsequent runs are faster.
- **The `babelConfig: true` flag** ensures the Babel transform pipeline (module-resolver, react-native-reanimated/plugin, etc.) still runs after ts-jest compiles TypeScript. This preserves the `~` alias resolution and all other Babel plugins.

**One potential edge case to watch:** If a future test file uses `import React from 'react'` at the top level AND uses JSX inside a `jest.mock()` factory, the same hoisting issue will resurface. The established pattern (use `require` + `React.createElement` inside factories) is well-documented in both test files and should be followed going forward.

---

### Summary

| Area | Status |
|---|---|
| ts-jest configuration | Correct -- `createJsWithBabelPreset` is the right preset for RN + TS + Babel |
| jest.mock() fixes | Correct -- canonical pattern for hoisted factories with ts-jest |
| tsconfig.spec.json | Correct -- minimal, clean override of `jsx` only |
| Transform coverage | Complete -- all file types properly routed |
| Regressions | None -- 113/113 tests pass, 0 new lint errors |

**APPROVED.**
